question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted record stream that causes prune() to be invoked with a future consensusTimestamp value, deleting all current transaction history and making the mirror node appear to have no historical record, thereby reorganizing the visible transaction timeline?
